title: SUPPORT GROUPS - 211info
text: SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support groups and community education on sexual harassment, sexual assault, and domestic violence and adult domestic violence prevention workshops. Provides medical, legal, and financial advocacy for survivors such as navigating the Department of Human Services Child Welfare and the Self Sufficiency office. Assists with applying for Temporary Assistance for Domestic Violence Survivors (TA-DVS). Rural outreach: Supports survivors with safety planning, support groups, and meets for weekly consultation. 2194 Columbia Boulevard Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and/or sexual violence, stalking, and sex trafficking Hours: Varies Email (503) 397-6161 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Crisis Line (503) 397-6161 Toll Free Crisis Line (866) 397-6161 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information. Rural advocacy is currently by appointment only in Clatskanie and Rainier. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Clatsop and Columbia County SITE HOURS: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, closed holidays Services: Advocacy * Domestic Violence Issues Advocacy * Sexual Assault Issues Domestic Violence Support Groups Sexual Assault Prevention Sexual Assault/Incest Support Groups Spouse/Intimate Partner Abuse Prevention Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - THE SAFE HOUSE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY
